title: Tiger Woods Arrested for DUI Two Weeks Before the Masters
excerpt: Tiger Woods arrested on suspicion of DUI after a Jupiter Island rollover crash — just 13 days before the 2026 Masters tees off at Augusta.
body:
A Shocking Turn Just Before Augusta

Two weeks before the 2026 Masters, Tiger Woods found himself at the centre of a crisis far removed from the fairways. On 27 March, officers from the Martin County Sheriff’s Office attended a rollover crash on Jupiter Island, Florida — and arrested Woods, 50, on suspicion of driving under the influence.

What Happened on Jupiter Island

Just before 2 p.m., Woods was driving a Range Rover along South Beach Road when he approached a pickup truck slowing to turn into a driveway. He attempted to pass, clipped the trailer the truck was towing, and the SUV rolled onto its driver’s side. Remarkably, nobody was injured. Woods climbed out through the passenger window unaided.

At the scene, Sheriff John Budensiek described Woods as “lethargic” and displaying “signs of impairment.” A breathalyser returned triple zeroes — no alcohol detected. Woods then refused to provide a urine sample. “He cooperated with the breathalyzer, and then the urine he wanted no part of,” Budensiek told reporters.

Three Misdemeanour Charges

Woods was charged with DUI, property damage, and refusal to submit to a lawful test. He spent roughly eight hours in Martin County Jail before being released on bond around 11 p.m. ET. Florida defence attorney Robert Reiff told Yahoo Sports the DUI charge may not hold — without a urinalysis, prosecutors face a high bar proving impairment by a controlled substance under state law.

Breathalyser Result
0.000
Hours in Custody
~8
Days to Masters
13
Prior DUI Arrests
2

Masters and Ryder Cup in the Balance

The crash landed 13 days before Augusta National hosts the Masters (April 9–12). President Trump, a close friend of Woods, told Fox News: “I love Tiger but he won’t be there. He’ll be there, but he won’t be playing in it.” Woods was also approaching a soft deadline from the PGA of America regarding the U.S. Ryder Cup captaincy for 2027 in Ireland — a role that now looks deeply uncertain.

Three Days Earlier: a Glimpse of the Old Tiger

The arrest came just three days after Woods captained Jupiter Links in the TGL Finals at the SoFi Center — his first competitive appearance since the 2024 Open Championship. Los Angeles Golf Club claimed the SoFi Cup 9–2, but Woods’s involvement drove viewership to nearly one million, the second-highest rating in TGL history. It felt like a genuine return. Now the sport is left asking very different questions about golf’s most compelling figure.
